ucs.title: [LLM] Confucius lure-doc chain: Office template injection / Equation Editor spawning mshta.exe or PowerShell to fetch remote payload
ucs.description: Hunts Confucius's signature initial-access-to-execution chain: a weaponised Office document (often Pakistan/India military or diplomatic-themed) abuses remote template injection (T1221) or CVE-2017-11882 in Equation Editor (eqnedt32.exe) to spawn mshta.exe or PowerShell with an external URL, downloading the next-stage RAT (Warzone, Quasar, or Confucius's bespoke loaders). This combination of Office parent + LOLBin child + outbound HTTP is the most consistent fingerprint of G0142 across TrendMicro/Uptycs reporting (2017-2021).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | where match(cmdline,"(?i)https?:\/\/|\\\\[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\") OR match(cmdline,"(?i)(DownloadString|IEX|Invoke-Expression|FromBase64String|-enc(?:odedcommand)?|hta:|javascript:|vbscript:)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let ScriptingChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let SuspiciousChain = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
    | where FileName in~ (ScriptingChildren)
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(https?://|\\\\[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\|hta:|javascript:|vbscript:|DownloadString|Invoke-Expression|IEX|FromBase64String|-enc(odedcommand)?\s)"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              Child = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ChildSHA256 = SHA256;
// Enrich with the immediate outbound connection from that child process within 2 minutes
SuspiciousChain
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(NetTime) or (NetTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 2m))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Parent, ParentCmd, Child, ChildCmd, ChildSHA256, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let ScriptingChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in (OfficeParents)
| where ChildBin in (ScriptingChildren)
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(https?://|\\\\[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\|hta:|javascript:|vbscript:|DownloadString|Invoke-Expression|IEX|FromBase64String|-enc(odedcommand)?\s)"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentBin, ParentProcessName, ChildBin, NewProcessName, CommandLine, NewProcessId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Confucius APT - Office Application Spawns Scripting LOLBin With Remote URL
id: 8f3c2b9c-5a4e-4f4a-9c2d-3a18a4e7e9d3
status: experimental
description: Detects the Confucius (G0142) initial-access chain where a weaponised Office document (template injection T1221 or CVE-2017-11882 via eqnedt32.exe) spawns mshta/PowerShell/wscript with an embedded remote URL or download primitive.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0142/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/21/h/confucius-uses-pegasus-spyware-related-lures-to-target-pakistani-military.html
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1221
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0142
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
  selection_payload:
    CommandLine|re|i:
      - 'https?://'
      - 'hta:'
      - 'javascript:'
      - 'vbscript:'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - '\bIEX\b'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-enc(odedcommand)?\s'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_payload
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise macros that fetch remote content (rare; should be allow-listed by hash)
  - IT-managed mshta-driven onboarding scripts launched from Office
level: high
ucs.rationale: Confucius's tradecraft is unusually consistent: TrendMicro (Feb 2018, Aug 2021) and Uptycs (Jan 2021) all document the same chain — RTF/DOCX lure exploiting CVE-2017-11882 in Microsoft Equation Editor (eqnedt32.exe) or using OOXML remote-template injection (T1221), which spawns mshta.exe / PowerShell to retrieve their second-stage loaders (sometimes Warzone or Quasar variants). The eqnedt32.exe pa

_field_issues.message: `Public` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`RemoteIP` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemoteUrl` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemotePort` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, ASCDeviceEvents.
`Parent` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Child` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ChildCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ChildSHA256` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `SHA256`?

ucs.title: [LLM] Confucius staged exfil: archive of user docs followed by HTTPS upload to public cloud storage by non-browser process
ucs.description: Hunts the Confucius collection-and-exfiltration tail: their custom RATs (and SunBird/Hornbill on mobile-adjacent endpoints) stage data into archives, then exfiltrate to legitimate web services (T1567.002) — Mega, Dropbox, OneDrive personal, Google Drive, anonfiles, transfer.sh — to blend with normal traffic. The actor specifically prefers cloud-storage exfil over custom C2 to evade egress filtering in South Asian government networks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app IN ("ssl","http","https") AND (All_Traffic.dest_host IN ("*.mega.nz","*.mega.co.nz","*.dropbox.com","*.dropboxusercontent.com","*.googleapis.com","*.anonfiles.com","*.transfer.sh","*.bashupload.com","*.gofile.io","*.pixeldrain.com","*.file.io","*.send.tresorit.com") OR All_Traffic.url IN ("*mega.nz*","*dropbox*","*anonfiles*","*transfer.sh*","*gofile.io*"))) by All_Traffic.src host All_Traffic.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("All_Traffic")` | where process_name!="" AND NOT match(process_name,"(?i)(chrome|msedge|firefox|brave|opera|iexplore|safari|onedrive|teams|outlook|onedrivestandaloneupdater)\.exe$") | join type=outer host process_name [| tstats `summariesonly` count as archive_count values(Filesystem.file_path) as staged_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.zip","*.rar","*.7z","*.cab","*.tar","*.gz") AND Filesystem.action="created" by Filesystem.dest host Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")` | rename Filesystem.dest as src] | where archive_count>=1 OR bytes_out>1048576 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let CloudExfilDomains = dynamic([
    "mega.nz","mega.co.nz","userstorage.mega.co.nz",
    "dropbox.com","content.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com",
    "anonfiles.com","bayfiles.com","transfer.sh","bashupload.com",
    "gofile.io","pixeldrain.com","file.io","send.tresorit.com",
    "upload.box.com","uguu.se","catbox.moe"
]);
let BrowserOrSyncClients = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","onedrivestandaloneupdater.exe","teams.exe","outlook.exe",
    "googledrivesync.exe","dropbox.exe","megasync.exe"
]);
// Step 1: archive staging on the host within the last 24h
let ArchiveStaging = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(zip|rar|7z|cab|tar|gz|tar\.gz|tgz)$"
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (BrowserOrSyncClients)
    | summarize ArchiveCount = count(),
                ArchiveSamples = make_set(FileName, 5),
                FirstStaged = min(Timestamp),
                LastStaged = max(Timestamp)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessAccountName
    | where ArchiveCount >= 1;
// Step 2: same non-browser process talks to a cloud-exfil host
let CloudUpload = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (BrowserOrSyncClients)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | extend MatchedDomain = tostring(extract(@"(?i)([a-z0-9-]+\.(?:mega\.(?:nz|co\.nz)|userstorage\.mega\.co\.nz|dropbox\.com|dropboxapi\.com|dropboxusercontent\.com|anonfiles\.com|bayfiles\.com|transfer\.sh|bashupload\.com|gofile\.io|pixeldrain\.com|file\.io|tresorit\.com|box\.com|uguu\.se|catbox\.moe))", 1, RemoteUrl))
    | where isnotempty(MatchedDomain)
    | summarize ConnCount = count(),
                Hosts = make_set(MatchedDomain, 5),
                FirstUpload = min(Timestamp),
                LastUpload = max(Timestamp)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
// Step 3: same process did both — that's the alert
ArchiveStaging
| join kind=inner CloudUpload on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where LastStaged < LastUpload          // staging happened before upload
| project DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Process = InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessHash = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          ArchiveCount, ArchiveSamples, FirstStaged, LastStaged,
          ConnCount, ExfilHosts = Hosts, FirstUpload, LastUpload,
          DwellSeconds = datetime_diff('second', LastUpload, LastStaged)
| order by LastUpload desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let CloudExfilHostRegex = @"(?i)([a-z0-9-]+\.(?:mega\.(?:nz|co\.nz)|dropbox\.com|dropboxapi\.com|dropboxusercontent\.com|anonfiles\.com|bayfiles\.com|transfer\.sh|bashupload\.com|gofile\.io|pixeldrain\.com|file\.io|tresorit\.com|box\.com|uguu\.se|catbox\.moe))";
let BrowserOrSyncClients = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","onedrivestandaloneupdater.exe","teams.exe","outlook.exe",
    "googledrivesync.exe","dropbox.exe","megasync.exe"
]);
let ArchiveStaging = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(zip|rar|7z|cab|tar|gz|tgz)$"
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | extend ActingBin = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ActingBin !in (BrowserOrSyncClients)
    | summarize ArchiveCount = count(),
                ArchiveSamples = make_set(TargetFileName, 5),
                LastStaged = max(TimeGenerated)
                by DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingBin;
let CloudUpload = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where isnotempty(Url)
    | where Url matches regex CloudExfilHostRegex
    | extend ActingBin = tolower(tostring(split(coalesce(SrcUsername, ""), "\\")[-1]))   // best-effort if process name unavailable
    | summarize ConnCount = count(),
                ExfilHost = make_set(extract(CloudExfilHostRegex, 1, Url), 5),
                LastUpload = max(TimeGenerated),
                BytesOut = sum(NetworkBytes)
                by DvcHostname, SrcUsername;
ArchiveStaging
| join kind=inner CloudUpload on DvcHostname
| where LastStaged < LastUpload
| project DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingBin,
          ArchiveCount, ArchiveSamples, LastStaged,
          ConnCount, ExfilHost, BytesOut, LastUpload,
          DwellSeconds = datetime_diff('second', LastUpload, LastStaged)
| order by LastUpload desc
ucs.rationale: Confucius's exfiltration TTP is documented across Uptycs and TrendMicro reports as preferring legitimate cloud storage (T1567.002) — Mega, anonfiles, Dropbox — because South Asian government targets often allow these as productivity domains while blocking exotic C2. The detection's high-fidelity hinge is requiring the SAME non-browser process to (a) write an archive locally and (b) push it to one
